text:
AI is increasingly common in the workplace, transforming how tasks are completed. Automation handles repetitive jobs like data entry, freeing employees to focus on more creative and strategic work. Many businesses now use productivity tools powered by AI, such as smart scheduling assistants and automated report generators. These tools help teams work faster and with fewer errors.

Rather than replacing humans entirely, AI encourages human-AI collaboration. For example, customer service agents use AI chatbots to handle simple questions, which allows them to spend more time on complex issues. This shift means some job roles change, requiring new skills like managing AI systems or interpreting data. While certain jobs may disappear, new opportunities are also created, and workers who adapt can find their roles becoming more engaging and valuable.
